rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD HAUSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: PES-19-302585 | ETATE OF RICHARD HAUSER 4/13/21 g 5/25/21 Continued to 5/25/21. (No earlier availability.) Notice must be given directly to a party entitled to notice, not just to the attorney for the party, unless the party entitled to notice has directed in writing that notice be given only to his attorney. [CRC 7.51(a).] A petition to determine entitlement to the estate must be filed prior to a petition for final distribution. [LRSF 14.36 H.] It appears from Joseph Mickolowski's last name that he must be a maternal cousin. That would not preclude paternal cousins from taking. It is unclear why the administrator now believes that the parties listed in her initial petition (the issue of one of Decedent's paternal aunts) are not blood relatives. Additionally, no further information has been filed regarding the dates of death or any surviving issue of the other four paternal aunts and uncles. Given that heirs have not yet been determined, any declaration regarding Probate Code Section 9202(c) appears to be premature. If the administrator had intended to request a reduction in bond, that was missing from the petition caption and the prayer. An amendment must be filed if the administrator wishes to have her bond reduced. If the Court were to grant another six months from the date the petition was signed and verified, that would result in an extension of time to just July 2021 Had the administrator intended to request an additional six months from the date of the Court's order? (The administrator was appointed on 3/26/19, not 3/27/19. Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS RICHARD GOIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/25/21. (No earlier availability.) Courtesy copies must be single-sided. [LRSF 14.7 B.1.] A proposed order must also be single-sided. A single-sided courtesy copy of the petition and any supplement thereto (signed and verified by the administrator) and a single-sided proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/14/21. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc., not just currently. A proposed order must state how the estate will be distributed within the body of the order, not in an attachment. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLLIE MAE MIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/5/2021 1) Petitioner was appointed with $150k bond. On 10/01/2020 when petitioner/attorney filed the Final I&A alleging $691,012.44 in assets Petitioner/attorney had a duty to apply for an increase in bond. [LRSF 14.16, CRC 7.204]. Matter continued for Petitioner/attorney to file an ex parte to increase bond and follow LRSF 14.11 B. 2) Please file withdrawals of each creditor claim or proof that the creditors deem each claim has been satisfied in full. 3) Please file receipts for the requests for reimbursement. 4) Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. (CRC 7.705). 5) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOEL ROBERT STODD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: PES-20-303666 | ESTATE OF JOEL ROBERT STODD Continued to 5/19/21. (No earlier availability.) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the cash on hand. Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.35 D.] The attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." Refunds that were for amounts paid during estate administration are not receipts includable in the fee base (such as refunds for property tax or income tax). An account must start with the property on hand at the beginning of the period covered by the account. [Probate Code Section 1061(a)(1).] The detailed supporting schedules must show the calculation, not just the net gain or loss. [Probate Code Section 1062(d).] Schedule F (Administrator's costs to be reimbursed) does not reflect dates or identify the original payee for each item of "MISC COSTS" and "Administration Papers" is not a helpful description. The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450. The initial Petition for Letters of Administration never identified the parents and their dates of death to confirm that they did, in fact, predecease the decedent. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] A petition is either SUPPLEMENTED to provide additional information or AMENDED to provide materially different information. The "Verified Declaration" filed on 2/18/21 purportedly supplementing the petition actually amended it; however, as a copy of it was reportedly served with the notice, the administrator will not be required to refile it as the amendment that it is. An AMENDMENT to the petition will be required, however, to address these issues. A courtesy copy of the amendment and proof of service of notice of the amendment must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/10/21. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS appointed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL AQUINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/2021. 1) Objections filed no required courtesy copy provided to Examiner. No competiting petition for probate filed. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) It appears a Will was lodged by Objector. Will does not appear to be original. 3) Petitioner has not filed a response to the Examiner's Notes: a. Petitioner did not select a box on page 1 under 1 and did not specify the name of the newspaper. b. On page 1 #2 under name of Petitioner is blank. c. Please file a copy of Decedent's death certificate. d. On page 1 under #2 c. petitioner did not check a box full or limited authority. e. On page 1 under #2 d. Petitioner selected bond not be required for the reasons stated in item 3e and then under 3e. petitioner did not select a box. f. On page 1 under #3.a Petitioner left place of death blank. g. On page 2 under 3. D. Petitioner states the personal property is $2,700,000.00 and it appears Petitioner alleges the annual gross income from real property is $6,000.00. Please explain why the subtotal adds up to $2,707,200.00. Please explain why gross fair market value of real property is blank if there is $6,000.00 in annual gross income from real property. Please explain why net value of real property is blank. h. Petitioner selected Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary and then selected on page 2 under #3 f. Decedent died intestate. Please explain. i. On page 2 under #3h petitioner did not select boxes 1 resident of California or 2 nonresident of California and 3 resident of United States or 4 nonresident of United States have not been selected. j. Please explain why Petitioner did not select a box on page 3 under #5 a (1) spouse or (2) no spouse) when there is a spouse listed on page 4 under #8. k. Please explain why Petitioner did not include an Attachment 3(f)(3) that includes a statement of the testamentary words or their substance and states reasons in that attachment why the presumption in Probate Code 6124 does not apply. l. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). m. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). n. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. o. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] Examiner received unfiled courtesy copies from Dorian Aquino in the mail on 4/9/2021 and they have been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHERMAN AND BERTHA BISHOP LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Trustee To Account
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearances required. petitioner must provide a proposed order to the court (by mail or drop box - in person appearance in the courtroom is not allowed absent exigent circumstances). Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DONALD L. YOST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining That Certain Assets Are Included In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar. Still no courtesy copy of the petition and no proof of service of the correct notice despite the two prior continuances. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THORDIS A. BARNES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the purpose of admitting the will dated 9/19/1970. No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set on 4/27/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 4/27/2022. Appearance will be required on 4/27/2022 if no petition has been filed.
